steps.title: Use the shift pattern
steps.content: The first five values in data set B are exactly $1$ greater than the first five values in data set A. Adding the same amount to every value does not change standard deviation.

steps.title: Check the three options for h
steps.content: If $h=28$, then all six values in B are A shifted up by $1$, so the standard deviations are equal and II does not work. If $h=23$, the last value is closer to the center than $27+1$, so B is less spread out than A and I works. If $h=29$, the last value is farther from the center than $27+1$, so B is more spread out than A and III does not work.

steps.title: Match the choice
steps.content: Only statement I could be true, so A is correct.
